summary: Verified the multi-image PDP fix in /app/frontend/src/lib/shopify.js (transformProduct: generic/untagged images now distributed to every variant). Tested all 5 required product handles on the LIVE preview URL at desktop 1400x900 across every colour variant. All acceptance criteria pass.

Antique Rose.note: variant has single image in Shopify; gallery hidden when length<=1

Python.note: Python main image = butterfly-python-2 (NOT antique rose) — earlier alt-text regression fix holds

agonda-scarf.note: Other colours only have 1 variant-specific image on Shopify

mariposa-scarf.note: Each colour has a single variant-tagged image; no generic images exist for this product — gallery correctly hides when only 1 image

critical_code_review_comments: shopify.js line 283-287: generic-image fan-out is O(images*variants). Acceptable for current catalog size but should be watched as catalog grows.
ProductDetail.jsx line 132 hides thumbnails when galleryImages.length<=1. This is correct behaviour; variants showing 0 thumbs simply have a single image on Shopify — not a bug.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Multi-image PDP fix verified live. Gallery thumbnails are conditionally rendered only when a variant has >1 image (ProductDetail.jsx:132). Do not misread '0 thumbnails' as a bug — check whether that variant actually has multiple images in Shopify first. Cart/checkout not re-tested per instructions.
rca of the issue: Original bug: transformProduct dropped images whose alt-text didn't match any variant colour. Fix appends such generic images to every variant. Verified on 5 products x 26 variants.
